mappings.rationale: Covers the Earth's concentric layers, seismic wave analysis, and the geophysical evidence used to infer interior structure and composition.
Provides a comprehensive treatment of the Earth's internal structure including crust, mantle, core, and the Mohorovicic discontinuity.
Covers the distinction between continental and oceanic crust, lithosphere and mantle structure, and subduction processes that reveal interior composition.
Covers the layered structure of the Earth, earthquake processes, and geochemical differentiation of the Earth's interior.
Directly relevant to the composition of the Earth's core, mantle, and crust—the basic divisions of the solid Earth discussed in section A.2.
Relevant to the mineral composition and phase transitions at depth, and the distribution of elements in the Earth's core, mantle, and crust.
Covers earthquakes—their causes, distribution, and magnitude—which are central to section B on seismic sources within the Earth.
Volcanism provides direct evidence about the Earth's interior composition, magma generation in the mantle, and the molten state of the outer core.
Covers the chemical elements and their properties, relevant to the distribution of elements in the Earth's core, mantle, and crust (section C).
Discusses the development of the Earth's structure and composition as an integrated system, relevant to section A.3 on the evolution of the Earth's interior.
Covers wave propagation and behavior, directly relevant to seismic wave velocities and their use in interpreting the Earth's interior structure (section D.1).
